name: Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop
name_original: Bages - La Corbetera, La Gavarresa, Artés, Las Tàpies - circular
description: This moderate 16.1-mile mountain biking loop near Artés offers a varied ride past Corbatera Pond and historical sites.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sallent is characterized by its diverse terrain and significant elevation changes, set within a mountainous environment. The region offers a network of trails that traverse varied landscapes, from forested paths to open tracks. Riders can expect challenging climbs and exhilarating descents across the area's natural features. This makes Sallent a destination for mountain bikers seeking varied experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Sallent The most popular mountain bike trail is Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Corbatera Riverside Area – Parque de l'Agulla loop from Sallent, a moderate 39.0 miles (62.8 km) path. This route follows the Corbatera Riverside Area and leads towards Parque de l'Agulla. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Montserrat – Parque de l'Agulla loop from Sallent, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail offering views of Montserrat and Parque de l'Agulla, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Sallent is defined by mountainous terrain, varied trails, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Sallent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from around 290 reviews. More than 3,800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sallent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sallent?
A: Sallent offers a network of over 30 mountain bike trails. These routes provide a diverse range of experiences, from easier paths to more challenging ascents and descents, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Sallent?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sallent feature diverse and mountainous terrain. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes traversing forested paths, rocky sections, and open tracks. The highest trailheads reach considerable elevations, offering challenging climbs and exhilarating descents characteristic of the Pyrenean landscape.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Sallent?
A: Yes, Sallent has options for beginners. There are 8 easy mountain bike trails available. These routes are generally less demanding in terms of elevation and technical skill, providing a good starting point for those new to mountain biking in the region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Sallent?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sallent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 300 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the significant elevation changes that offer both challenging climbs and rewarding descents, and the stunning mountainous environment.

Q: Are there any difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Sallent offers 8 difficult mountain bike trails. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31776471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cal Pinyot – Burgaroles Farmhouse loop from la Botjosa&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain and covers a distance of over 38 km.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many trails in Sallent provide scenic viewpoints due to the region's mountainous nature. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33759828" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montserrat – Parque de l'Agulla loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt; offers views of Montserrat and the surrounding landscape. High elevations typically provide panoramic vistas of the Pyrenean landscape.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking in Sallent, you can encounter several natural attractions. Notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de l'Agulla&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of several routes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3256464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bigaire Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3478264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baga Cerdana Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4018958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collbaix Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Sallent?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Sallent are designed as loops. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955795851/sender-de-la-gavarra-and-artes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 25.9 km with diverse landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33795640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corbatera Riverside Area – Parque de l'Agulla loop from Sallent&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 62.8 km path.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sallent?
A: The mountainous environment of Sallent suggests that spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and stable conditions. Summer can also be suitable, especially for higher elevation routes, but be mindful of potential heat and increased visitor numbers. Winter may present challenges due to snow and colder temperatures.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, Sallent offers several longer routes for those looking for extended rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17564183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chapel of Sant Marc – Sant Vicenç d'Obiols Hermitage loop from Poblat Ibèric del Cogulló&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route spanning over 87 km with significant elevation gain, perfect for a full day's adventure.

Q: Are there any specific considerations for mountain biking in Sallent regarding other users or livestock?
A: When mountain biking in Sallent, it's important to be mindful of local conditions. The area is also used for livestock grazing and sees a high influx of mountaineers and hikers, particularly in summer. Please respect animals and other users, and remember to close any gates you pass through to maintain the tranquility and safety of the environment.
tips: A beautiful area of marshes next to the Llobregat.
Torrecabota is an old farmhouse converted into a rural hotel. There are excellent views throughout the estate.
A technical trail with some rough parts but completely manageable.

Les Tàpies is a farmhouse in the municipality of Calders, in Moianès. The primitive one was not located in the place of the present farmhouse, but near her. The Tàpies family is documented as early as 1290, probably the place name derives from the type of material in which the house was built. In 1880 a chapel was built there under the invocation of the Virgin Immaculate.

The Pont de Cabrianes factory was built at the end of the 19th century, next to the bridge that crosses the Llobregat and near the Mas Bertran. Joaquim Bertran Calderó built a neighborhood of housing for workers, a hostel, a parish church and a school. Later, it passed into the hands of the Berenguer family, who were the owners of three factories in Artés, Cabrianes and Pont de Cabrianes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking this loop is around 2 hours and 9 minutes. This is an average and can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop is graded as moderate. While specific detailed terrain information for this exact loop is not readily available, a moderate rating typically indicates some challenging sections, possibly with varied surfaces, ascents, and descents that require a reasonable level of fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific detailed terrain information for this exact loop is not widely documented, given its 'moderate' difficulty and location in the Artés region, you can generally expect a mix of dirt tracks, gravel paths, and potentially some singletrack sections. The route features an elevation gain of approximately 301 meters, so you will encounter climbs and descents.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop, you'll encounter several interesting points. These include the tranquil Corbatera Pond, the historic Torrecabota Country House and Chapel, and the scenic Torrent Tàpies Trail. You can also see the Les Tàpies Farmhouse and the Pont de Cabrianes Factory Ruins, offering glimpses into the area's history.

faqs.question: Does the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. Notably, it follows parts of the Sender de la Gavarra, Camí Ramader de Marina, Camí Ral de Manresa a Vic, and Sender d'Artés. You'll also find smaller segments of trails like the Camí del Llobregat and Camí de Sant Jaume.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Artés region generally offers good mountain biking conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter weather. Always check the local forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Be sure to carry water for your pet, as specific water sources on the trail are not guaranteed.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Generally, for trails like the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop in this region, permits or entrance fees are not required for mountain biking. However, it's always a good practice to respect private property and any local signage you may encounter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sender de la Gavarra and Artés Loop?
faqs.answer: Specific trailhead parking for this exact loop is not detailed in available information. However, Artés is a likely starting point, and you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town. It's advisable to check local maps or satellite views for suitable parking areas near the beginning of the loop.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: Artés, being a local town, will offer various amenities. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options within or very close to Artés, which serves as a convenient base for this loop. It's a good idea to check local listings for current operating hours.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point of the loop?
faqs.answer: Artés is generally accessible by public transport, typically bus services connecting it to larger towns or cities in the Bages region. You would need to check local bus schedules and routes to plan your journey to Artés, and then cycle to the loop's starting point from there.
